steps.dialogue: Have you ever noticed [event:68fa814c7bc1bc78] how some things can be split [event:695fd80398d354fb] into parts that are exactly the same size? Like when you break [event:68fa8195dde38993] a chocolate bar along its lines. Today, you're going to learn about [event:68fa81feb0642525] [vocab]equal parts[/vocab] and [vocab]fractions[/vocab].

Look at these [event:68fa82c5b1d78a6e]three bars on your screen. These bars help us see how things can be split into parts. Each bar is the same size [event:68fa848b2d1cd543]but they're divided differently. Did you notice that one bar is split into parts that are [event:68fa84f80b8fdbad] exactly the same size? Take a close look and click the bar where all parts match perfectly.

prompt.text: Click on the bar where all parts are the same size.

step.dialogue: Look again at the parts in the bars. They all need to be the same width.
You're working on it. [event:68fa85c329417d9b] Look at how the parts compare in size. See how they don't match perfectly? We need all parts to be exactly the same width.
I can help show you. [event:68fa87fe100e6619]Look at the middle bar - [event:68fa878b5db24425] see how each part is exactly the same width? That's what equal parts look like.

on_correct.dialogue: Right. [event:68fa88cd7003a4bd]You noticed that all the parts in that bar are exactly the same size.

steps.dialogue: Let's look closer - can you click one part to highlight it?

prompt.text: Click one part of the bar to highlight it.

step.dialogue: Not quite. Try clicking directly on just one of the four parts to shade it in.
[event:68fa89c7e793d332]We want to shade in one of the four parts. Try clicking on only one section.
[event:690d02285fdf7599]Let's work through this together: [event:690d02ba5fbde4c9]I click on one of these four equal parts to shade it. See how it changes color?

on_correct.dialogue: That's it. See how that part [event:68fa8b21a50a9fd7]is exactly the same size as the others in its bar?

steps.dialogue:
[event:68fa8bc5e2670d70] When we split something into parts [event:68fa8bfa4ad2944a] that are all the same size, mathematicians call these
 [vocab] EQUAL PARTS. [/vocab]

[event:68fa8d74ae210755] When the parts are equal, every part [event:68fa8e4dbf9a7d71] is exactly the same size as the others. Some bars have more parts, some have fewer, but the important thing is that the parts in each bar match perfectly!
